chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ عَلَى مَنْ أَقَرَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ بِحَدٍّ وَ مَنْ لَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 34 – What Penalty (Hadd) is Obligated upon the one who accepts upon himself (confesses), and the one upon whom the Penalty (Hadd) is not Obligated

text: السَّارِقُ إِذَا جَاءَ مِنْ قِبَلِ نَفْسِهِ تَائِباً إِلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ رَدَّ سَرِقَتَهُ عَلَى صَاحِبِهَا فَلَا قَطْعَ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The thief, when he comes from his own self, repentant to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic, and returns the stolen goods to its owner, so there is not cutting (of the hand) upon him’.
